content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

Amendment fr-14-c8de46bb identifies a genuine validation gap: once all expected canonical sections have been seen (expected_index == len), the validator at line 252 short-circuits because .get(expected_index) returns None, silently allowing any duplicate or extra canonical heading after the last section. Since AGENTS/Repo Guidance is the last section and its content is emitted verbatim at top level, a drifted prompt can append duplicate canonical headings that bypass drift detection. Amendment fr-14-015e8372 describes an intentional design choice — task_prompt_contract is optional to allow legacy/custom overrides. The canonical prompt still flows through project_prompt/prompt_text, and canonical-shape validation runs post-generation, so this is a quality/compatibility tradeoff rather than a correctness bug.

## Votes

- `fr-14-c8de46bb`: Accept
  The validation gap is concrete and verifiable. Once expected_index advances past the last element (SECTION_AGENTS_REPO_GUIDANCE at index 7), BEAD_TASK_PROMPT_SECTION_TITLES.get(expected_index) returns None, so the error-reporting branch at lines 252-258 never fires for any canonical heading that appears after all expected sections have been consumed. This means a prompt containing a duplicate `## Acceptance Criteria` or `## Must-Do Scope` after `## AGENTS / Repo Guidance` will pass validate_canonical_prompt_shape() without error. Combined with the fact that service.rs emits repo guidance content verbatim (which could itself contain markdown headings matching canonical titles), this creates a real false-negative path for contract drift detection. The fix — rejecting trailing canonical headings after the contract block is complete — is straightforward and doesn't break valid prompts.
- `fr-14-015e8372`: Reject
  Making task_prompt_contract optional is a deliberate compatibility choice, not a defect. The builders always supply the value (engine.rs:165, prompt_review.rs, completion.rs, final_review.rs), so only custom workspace/project template overrides that explicitly omit the {{task_prompt_contract}} placeholder would lose the guidance. Critically, the canonical project prompt itself still flows through {{project_prompt}} or {{prompt_text}} — both of which are required placeholders — and canonical-shape validation still runs on the generated output. The omitted content is supplementary consumer guidance (explaining how to interpret the contract sections), not the contract itself. Forcing the placeholder would break any existing workspace override that doesn't include it, and injecting guidance outside the template body would undermine the override mechanism's design intent. This is a reasonable quality/compatibility tradeoff.
